summary.intent: Build a browser-in-browser feature for the moltis agent platform, allowing users to view and interact with browser sessions created by agents, and create their own sessions from the UI.
summary.outcome: Delivered a full browser viewing/interaction feature: live CDP screencast with mouse/keyboard/scroll/paste support, URL autocomplete bar, session history with action logs in SQLite, per-agent browser profiles with cookie persistence, and a 'New Session' UI button with profile selection.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService had separate BrowserManager instances — sharing required Arc<OnceCell> pattern matching how other singletons are wired
The gateway uses a two-phase init: services are created early (line ~1321 in server.rs), but db_pool comes later (line ~1497), requiring deferred store wiring via set_session_store()
WebSocket event subscription is explicit — clients must declare event types in websocket.js subscribeEvents array or broadcast.rs filter drops them silently
Browser CDP screencast relay task exits on tx.send() with zero receivers — must drop frames instead of exiting to avoid race with late subscribers
axum 0.8 uses {param} syntax for path captures, not :param
The workspace denies .expect() in tests via clippy deny — use assert!() or unwrap_or_else pattern instead
Two biome versions coexist: npx biome (v0.3.3, old) vs system biome (v2.4.6) — local-validate.sh uses system biome, causing format drift if wrong version is used

code.finding: BrowserRequest serde uses #[serde(default)] which handles missing fields but NOT explicit null — LLMs send null for all optional fields, requiring obj.retain(|_,v| !v.is_null()) strip before deserialization
Original relay task exited when tx.send() found zero receivers (LaggingReceivers/NoReceivers error) — must use SendError check only, drop frames on no-receiver condition
CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent mouseWheel requires both deltaX AND deltaY to always be present — omitting either (even when zero) causes 'deltaX and deltaY are expected' error
sandbox_profile_dir() was per-session; shared profile requires SingletonLock deletion before container launch to prevent Chrome startup failure
Canvas event listeners attached via useEffect([]) miss the canvas when it renders conditionally — use ref callback (canvasRefCallback) pattern to attach on DOM insertion
browser.screencast.frame was missing from the subscribeEvents list — broadcast.rs filter silently drops all frames for unsubscribed event types
CDP keyboard events require windowsVirtualKeyCode for special keys (Backspace=8, Enter=13, arrows=37-40, Delete=46, Escape=27) — without it Chrome silently ignores them
Viewport resize via SetDeviceMetricsOverride on every start_screencast call destabilizes Chrome containers — should only resize once per session using ScreencastRegistry.is_active() guard

workflow: YubiKey tap required for every git push — sign-once-per-session not available
local-validate.sh 531 is the validation harness for PRs; runs biome, fmt, i18n parity, install name checks, and zizmor in parallel
Background task notifications require reading output files via path in task-notification tag
CDP screencast frame chain: Chrome -> CDP event -> ScreencastRegistry broadcast -> service subscribe -> WS relay -> JS event listener -> canvas rAF draw — each link can silently drop frames
Integration tests for browser features need --test-threads=1 to avoid profile lock conflicts between parallel tests

friction: YubiKey tap required for every git push, causing repeated push failures mid-session
biome format --write via npx used wrong version (0.3.3 vs 2.4.6), causing repeated CI failures after apparently successful local lint
Multiple silent failure modes in the CDP → WS chain made debugging 'waiting for first frame' extremely difficult — required adding diagnostic logging at each step
Two separate BrowserManager instances (tool vs service) was an architectural issue that wasn't obvious from the codebase — no tests caught it
Gateway db_pool initialization order prevented clean wiring of BrowserSessionStore — required an awkward deferred set_session_store() pattern
Chrome containers crash when viewport resize (SetDeviceMetricsOverride) is called repeatedly on screencast start/stop cycles during session switching
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko-style) for lower latency and better bandwidth efficiency vs CDP JPEG screencast — noted as right long-term direction
Binary WebSocket frames for screencast (eliminates 33% base64 overhead) — identified but not implemented
Session history UI only shows action logs; no video/screenshot replay of what was done in a session
Agent-created browser sessions may not appear in session history if they bypass the service layer (BrowserTool goes directly to manager, not through RealBrowserService logging)
Multiple concurrent sandbox sessions sharing a single profile directory may have cookie write conflicts
Per-agent profile names use session_key directly — no config mapping for human-readable profile names
Scrollbar click-to-scroll uses CDPevaluate with scrollTo — not tested for reliability across all page types
Google autocomplete suggestions use suggestqueries.google.com — may have CORS issues in some network configurations
Playwright e2e tests for browser interaction (click accuracy, scroll) are written but not run in CI — marked as manual/integration tests
